name: CONTRACT-BACKLOG-17
title: Rabbit user-project plugin architecture (per-project .rabbit/, fully committed, vendored)
description:
## Source

Design doc: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-18-rabbit-user-project-plugin-architecture-design.md

This backlog item tracks implementation of the per-project plugin architecture for rabbit. The full design (motivation, choices, flows, deferrals, scope, brainstorming history) lives in the design doc above. This summary captures the load-bearing decisions and scope so the item is self-contained for triage.

## Goal

Extend rabbit to manage user projects — not just self-evolve. A user should be able to install rabbit into any project (greenfield or brownfield), use rabbit's TDD/contract machinery on that project's code, and ship work via the user's existing git workflow.

## Load-bearing architectural choices

1. **Per-project plugin model.** Rabbit installs at <user-project>/.rabbit/. 1-1 rule: one rabbit process targets one project. No central rabbit installation; no registry; no /rabbit-project switch command. Parallel work = multiple terminals in different .rabbit/ dirs.

2. **.rabbit/ fully committed, pure vendoring.** No embedded .git inside .rabbit/. Internal .gitignore excludes ephemerals (locks, scope markers, scratch). Specs/registry/items travel with code via the user's normal git push. git clone of the user repo gives a complete rabbit-enabled checkout — no bootstrap on clone.

3. **Co-located bookkeeping.** rabbit-project/ (specs, registry, items) lives inside .rabbit/ alongside the runtime in .rabbit/.claude/. Both committed.

4. **Meta-contract owns the structure.** New top-level contract declares: project signature (.rabbit/ in ancestry), required files and fixed locations (no per-project configurability), per-path commit policy, per-version migration scripts, owner/version. Single source of truth.

5. **Two operating modes.** Plugin mode (.rabbit/ in cwd ancestry → operate on parent as user project). Standalone mode (no .rabbit/ → operate on cwd as rabbit-self being edited). Same machinery; disambiguated by filesystem detection at session start.

6. **Update flow: fetch-diff-check-apply-revert.** Rabbit checks upstream on session start, banner if newer. User approves → fetch to temp, diff vs current .rabbit/ excluding rabbit-project/ and ephemerals, apply in place, run per-version migration scripts. Pass: explicit commit (chore(rabbit): update to X) for bisectability. Fail: revert; nothing changed.

7. **Concurrency.** Multi-rabbit same project: file locks on contracts (allowed with warning). Multi-user same project: git's job; merge conflicts on contracts at push time.

8. **Ship-it.** Rabbit commits + pushes user repo on user request, only when feature is at TDD step TEST-GREEN. Branch model / PRs / releases are the user's call.

## Scope of work

Touches features: contract (meta-contract is the keystone), rabbit-cage (new user-facing commands/skills for bootstrap, update, switch-mode-detection).

Likely also touches (during implementation):
- tdd-subagent: test command lookup must read project-map.json instead of assuming rabbit-self defaults
- rabbit-feature-touch: scope guard logic must work for both plugin and standalone modes
- rabbit-spec: spec authoring path resolution under both modes
- rabbit-file: item paths must work in both modes
- policy: may need updates if the philosophy/spec-rules/coding-rules implications are non-trivial

## Out of scope (v1)

- Imposing branch model on user projects
- Push/pull user code beyond bootstrap/ship-it/update
- Cross-project dependency management
- Multi-version rabbit runtime in one process (per-project pinning makes this unnecessary)
- Submodule-based vendoring (rejected; pure file vendoring chosen)
- Activity log / project-history recap
- Brownfield auto-discovery of feature boundaries
- Multi-user coordination beyond what git provides
- Central registry / "list my projects" meta-utility

## Open implementation questions (for the plan phase)

1. Exact path layout inside .rabbit/.claude/ vs today's structure
2. test-command field name in project-map.json and override precedence
3. Bootstrap script: shell one-liner vs rabbit-shipped bootstrap.sh
4. Session-start version-check mechanism
5. Migration script discovery (directory scan vs manifest)
6. Update skill name (/rabbit-config update vs new /rabbit-update)

## Status

Design approved 2026-05-18 (brainstorming session). Awaiting writing-plans skill to produce the implementation plan. Implementation deferred — this backlog item is for tracking, not immediate work.

history.note: Deferred: too large/complex for housekeeping waves. Requires dedicated design cycle. Filed for future roadmap consideration.
design completed and merged (PR #116, commit 839d5f7); reopening for implementation tracking (closed in error by long-running housekeeping session)
Deferred: per-project plugin architecture is a major roadmap item, not a cleanup task. Requires dedicated spec and design cycle.
design completed and merged (PR #116, commit 839d5f7); housekeeping closure was a context-drift error
Temporarily closed before CONTRACT-BACKLOG-19 housekeeping wave to prevent accidental closure by wave b/b filings; will be reopened after the wave completes

User-interfacer cycle complete — all 12 plan tasks delivered across 9 squash-merged PRs. The new design.md (4e2883aa) + plan.md (9e46471d) describe the lean-wedge architecture; PRs #201-#209 implement it. Drift-protected Claude is live in plugin mode via .rabbit/CLAUDE.md; opt-in feature mapping via rabbit-feature-new <name> <path-glob>; scope-guard blocks declared-feature edits without active marker; one-shot bypass via .rabbit/.runtime/scope-bypass-once. Install ritual documented in docs/install.md. TDD-on-user-code and B/B-on-user-code remain deferred per design v0.2.0.
